items.quote: To give anything less than your best is to sacrifice the gift.
items.author: Steve Prefontaine

source.title: Steve Prefontaine - Wikiquote
source.note: Attributed to Tom Jordan, Pre: America's Greatest Running Legend, Steve Prefontaine.

items.quote: I was not talented enough to run and smile at the same time.
items.author: Emil Zatopek
items.authorInfo: Czech long-distance runner and Olympic champion

source.title: Emil Zatopek - Wikiquote
source.note: Attributed by Mike Rowbottom, The Independent, 23 November 2000.

items.quote: Are you running out of breath? Go faster!
items.author: Emil Zatopek
items.authorInfo: Czech long-distance runner and Olympic champion

source.title: Emil Zatopek - Wikiquote
source.note: Listed as a life credo translated from Czech.

items.quote: Only the disciplined ones are free in life.
items.author: Eliud Kipchoge
items.authorInfo: Kenyan marathon runner and Olympic champion

source.title: Eliud Kipchoge - Wikiquote
source.note: Cited from Eliud Kipchoge & David Bedford at the Oxford Union, 5 January 2018.

items.author: Eliud Kipchoge
items.authorInfo: First person to run a marathon distance under two hours

source.title: The incredible science behind Eliud Kipchoge's 1:59 marathon
source.note: Reported after the INEOS 1:59 Challenge in Vienna, 12 October 2019.

items.quote: I knew if I quit, nobody would ever believe that women had the capability to run 26-plus miles.
items.author: Kathrine Switzer
items.authorInfo: First woman to officially run the Boston Marathon

source.title: Kathrine Switzer - Wikiquote
source.note: Quoted from Switzer's account of the 1967 Boston Marathon.

items.quote: I love how the simplicity of long distance running offers clarity to some of life's more complicated issues.
items.author: Deena Kastor
items.authorInfo: Olympic bronze medalist and American long-distance runner

source.title: How To Get Strong Marathoner's Legs Like Olympian Deena Kastor
source.note: Interview quote published by SELF, 1 November 2013.
